name: Kangaroo Tail Soup (in Pressure Cooker)
recipeInstructions:
1. Separate the joints of the kangaroo tail.
2. Brown the butter on a medium heat in the pressure cooker.
3. Quickly fry the tail pieces until they are lightly browned.
4. Add onions and carrots, and gently fry for 2 minutes.
5. Add celery, pepper, cloves, nutmeg, and salt; fry for another 10 minutes.
6. Stir in soy sauce and cook on high heat for 2 minutes.
7. Add tomatoes with liquid and stir to crush the tomatoes.
8. Pour water into the pot to cover all ingredients; stir well.
9. Put the lid on the pressure cooker and start timing when steam begins to escape.
10. After 25 minutes, turn off the heat and let it cool before removing the lid.
11. If you want a thicker soup, mix cornflour or coconut cream powder with hot water and stir into the soup; simmer for another 10 minutes without the lid.
Serve with rice.
